mission.title: surrealdb: Refactor transaction, caching, and key-value store interfaces
mission.description:
Thank you for submitting this pull request! We really appreciate you spending the time to work on these changes.

## What does this change do?

This pull-request introduces a major refactor of the transaction, caching, and key-value store layer. It introduces a number of improvements which enhance stability and performance, and allow for more improved code usage and behaviour. The major improvements include:

resolution.summary:
## What is the motivation?

Out of the concern for #1717, I realized there is a lot of duplicated code in `kvs` crate and I think it is better to clean it up than later. 

Use lesser code to do more stuff. How cool is that?

## What does this change do?

1. Add `DatastoreFacade`  for abstracting datastore (however, whether to name it this way can be controversial, given that it only has one `transaction` method that creates a boxed transaction facade object)
2. Add `TransactionFacade` for abstracting transactional actions
3. Add `DatastoreMetadata` that provides datastore metadata information
4. Store the metadata in as a global `AVAILABLE_DATASTORE_METADATA`
5. Use `AVAILABLE_DATASTORE_METADATA` to do self-introspection to reduce boilerplates in some key part of the code

In before you need to copy and paste 30+ places to add a KV datastore properly. Now you only needed 5.

This PR also added new private features `has-local`, `has-remote` and `has-tls` to the library crate, and I replaced a lot of `any(feature)` boilerplates as well. However, this could impact documentation reading, since far as I remember cargo docs are not feature-transitive.

## What is your testing strategy?

Just run the test suite. This is a modest PR that does not break the API compatibility too much (but all the internal KV datastore implementation does have breaking API changes -- I don't think any previous behavior would be changed if at all any.

Some may concern that the use of `async_trait` and

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with surrealdb installed or the issue environment reproducible.
